methodsAttempted.query: site:uow.edu.au University of Wollongong generative AI ChatGPT artificial intelligence students assessment academic integrity
methodsAttempted.notes: Firecrawl search found the official UOW Gen AI in assessment page, student academic-skills guidance, L&T Hub AI FAQ, an official HDR PDF, and research/news/showcase pages.

methodsAttempted.query: site:uow.edu.au UOW generative AI policy students assessment privacy copyright ChatGPT
methodsAttempted.notes: Firecrawl search confirmed the same official assessment and student guidance pages and surfaced official media/showcase pages that were not used as policy evidence.

methodsAttempted.query: site:policies.uow.edu.au generative AI artificial intelligence academic integrity policy UOW
methodsAttempted.notes: Firecrawl search found official Policy Directory pages for Academic Integrity, Academic Misconduct Procedure, Assessment and Feedback, Examination Procedure, and Subject Delivery.

methodsAttempted.query: site:uow.edu.au OR site:policies.uow.edu.au "generative artificial intelligence" "University of Wollongong" "personal information" "confidential"
methodsAttempted.notes: A follow-up Firecrawl search failed with a 402 credit error before returning results; direct HTTP fetches were used for verified official pages.
robots.txt was fetched from www.uow.edu.au and did not disallow the official guidance paths used in this bundle.
policies.uow.edu.au/robots.txt returned the public Policy Directory page rather than a robots file; the public policy pages were fetched respectfully with a crawler user agent.
ltc.uow.edu.au/robots.txt returned a 404 page; the public L&T Hub AI FAQ was fetched respectfully with a crawler user agent.

artifacts.summary: Discovery found official UOW guidance and policy pages with direct evidence about GenAI use in assessment, subject-specific permission, acknowledgement, Copilot privacy posture, academic misconduct, and staff handling of student work. News, showcase, and HDR-specific sources were documented but not used for general coursework or university-wide claims.

artifacts.claimText: UOW tells students to check the Subject Outline or subject Moodle site before using GenAI in an assessment task, because those sources specify the permitted extent of GenAI use and acknowledgement instructions.

artifacts.claimText: UOW states that misusing GenAI in assessments includes use where it is prohibited or beyond assessment instructions, and that misuse constitutes academic misconduct under University policy.

artifacts.claimText: UOW's student guidance recommends Microsoft Copilot when students use GenAI and says Copilot's Enterprise Data Protection means prompts and responses are encrypted and not retained by Microsoft to train its products.

Approved because the claim tracks the page's misuse and misconduct wording without expanding consequences.

Approved as a formal policy claim about Subject Outline minimum requirements.

Approved because the claim closely follows the Academic Integrity Policy's listed misuse categories.

artifacts.title: University of Wollongong GenAI Policy Evidence Draft
artifacts.summary: Staged draft based on official UOW sources: GenAI use in assessment is handled through subject-specific instructions, misuse can constitute academic misconduct, Subject Outlines must state whether and how GenAI may be used, staff may not upload student work to third-party GenAI tools, and student guidance recommends Copilot for its stated Enterprise Data Protection posture.
limitations: This tracker is not legal advice, not academic integrity advice, and not an official university statement unless a linked source is the university's own official page.
The L&T Hub FAQ claim is educator-facing guidance, not a formal policy mandate.
The HDR-specific PDF was rejected for broad coursework claims and should be reviewed separately if HDR coverage is needed.
Firecrawl search was available for initial discovery, but a later Firecrawl search returned a 402 credit error; verified official pages were fetched by direct HTTP.
artifacts.sourceRightsPolicy: Tracker metadata is open licensed. Official source documents, page text, PDFs, and other source materials retain their original rights and terms.
